root_causes.cause: Router R1 is dropping ICMP from the 172.16.0.0/24 network on its serial link to R2 (access-list R1_IN applied inbound on Serial0/1/0), preventing ICMP replies from 172.16.0.11 reaching the source 192.168.0.11.
evidence: R1 running-config (interface Serial0/1/0): ip.access-group.in.acl.acl-name = "R1_IN" (access-group applied inbound on the R1<->R2 link).

R1 access-lists: name "R1_IN" sequence 10 = action: "deny", protocol: "icmp", ipv4-address: "172.16.0.0", mask: "0.0.0.255", dst-eq-port2: "echo" (ACL explicitly denies ICMP matching the 172.16.0.0/24 network).

Control plane/routing is healthy between R1 and R2 (OSPF neighbors on Serial0/1/0 are 'ospf-nbr-full' and both routers advertise their LAN networks), making an ACL drop the most likely cause rather than a routing failure.

data.intent_description: Verify and diagnose network reachability failure: 25 ICMP pings sent from source 192.168.0.11 to destination 172.16.0.11 with 0 successful responses; determine cause and required remediation.

root_causes.cause: Router R1 is dropping ICMP from the 172.16.0.0/24 network on its serial link to R2 (access-list R1_IN applied inbound on Serial0/1/0), preventing ICMP replies from 172.16.0.11 reaching the source 192.168.0.11.
evidence: R1 running-config (interface Serial0/1/0): ip.access-group.in.acl.acl-name = "R1_IN" (access-group applied inbound on the R1<->R2 link).

R1 access-lists: name "R1_IN" sequence 10 = action: "deny", protocol: "icmp", ipv4-address: "172.16.0.0", mask: "0.0.0.255", dst-eq-port2: "echo" (ACL explicitly denies ICMP matching the 172.16.0.0/24 network).

Control plane/routing is healthy between R1 and R2 (OSPF neighbors on Serial0/1/0 are 'ospf-nbr-full' and both routers advertise their LAN networks), making an ACL drop the most likely cause rather than a routing failure.

data.intent_description: ICMP from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 is being blocked by ACL 'R1_IN' on router1 (sequence 10 denies ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 on Serial0/1/0). Goal: allow ICMP so pings succeed.

plan.problem: Router R1 (router1) has access-list 'R1_IN' applied inbound on Serial0/1/0 with sequence 10 denying ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24, preventing ICMP replies to 192.168.0.11.
plan.fix_summary: Modify ACL 'R1_IN' on router1 to permit ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 on the Serial0/1/0 inbound interface by replacing the denying entry (sequence 10) with a permit for ICMP.

messages.content:
{
  "intent": "check_and_fix",
  "intent_description": "ICMP from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 is being blocked by ACL 'R1_IN' on router1 (sequence 10 denies ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 on Serial0/1/0). Goal: allow ICMP so pings succeed.",
  "target": "router1",
  "needs_fix": true,
  "plan": {
    "problem": "Router R1 (router1) has access-list 'R1_IN' applied inbound on Serial0/1/0 with sequence 10 denying ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24, preventing ICMP replies to 192.168.0.11.",
    "fix_summary": "Modify ACL 'R1_IN' on router1 to permit ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 on the Serial0/1/0 inbound interface by replacing the denying entry (sequence 10) with a permit for ICMP.",
    "plan_steps": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "device": "router1",
        "action": "replace_acl_entry",
        "target": "access-list R1_IN",
        "parameters": [
          "sequence:10",
          "original_action:deny",
          "new_action:permit",
          "protocol:icmp",
          "src:172.16.0.0",
          "src_mask:0.0.0.255",
          "dst:any",
          "applied_interface:Serial0/1/0",
          "direction:inbound"
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}

plan.fix_summary: Modify ACL 'R1_IN' on router1 to permit ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 on the Serial0/1/0 inbound interface by replacing the denying entry (sequence 10) with a permit for ICMP.

root_causes.cause: Router R1 is dropping ICMP from the 172.16.0.0/24 network on its serial link to R2 (access-list R1_IN applied inbound on Serial0/1/0), preventing ICMP replies from 172.16.0.11 reaching the source 192.168.0.11.
evidence: R1 running-config (interface Serial0/1/0): ip.access-group.in.acl.acl-name = "R1_IN" (access-group applied inbound on the R1<->R2 link).

R1 access-lists: name "R1_IN" sequence 10 = action: "deny", protocol: "icmp", ipv4-address: "172.16.0.0", mask: "0.0.0.255", dst-eq-port2: "echo" (ACL explicitly denies ICMP matching the 172.16.0.0/24 network).

Control plane/routing is healthy between R1 and R2 (OSPF neighbors on Serial0/1/0 are 'ospf-nbr-full' and both routers advertise their LAN networks), making an ACL drop the most likely cause rather than a routing failure.

evidence.detail: router1 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.1) sees neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.2; router2 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.2) sees neighbor 1.1.1.1 @10.0.0.1
router1 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.10) sees neighbor 3.3.3.3 @10.0.0.9; router3 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.9) sees neighbor 1.1.1.1 @10.0.0.10

data.intent_description: ICMP from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 is being blocked by ACL 'R1_IN' on router1 (sequence 10 denies ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 on Serial0/1/0). Goal: allow ICMP so pings succeed.

evidence.detail: router1 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.1) sees neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.2; router2 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.2) sees neighbor 1.1.1.1 @10.0.0.1

root_causes.cause: Router R1 is dropping ICMP from the 172.16.0.0/24 network on its serial link to R2 (access-list R1_IN applied inbound on Serial0/1/0), preventing ICMP replies from 172.16.0.11 reaching the source 192.168.0.11.
evidence: R1 running-config (interface Serial0/1/0): ip.access-group.in.acl.acl-name = "R1_IN" (access-group applied inbound on the R1<->R2 link).

R1 access-lists: name "R1_IN" sequence 10 = action: "deny", protocol: "icmp", ipv4-address: "172.16.0.0", mask: "0.0.0.255", dst-eq-port2: "echo" (ACL explicitly denies ICMP matching the 172.16.0.0/24 network).

Control plane/routing is healthy between R1 and R2 (OSPF neighbors on Serial0/1/0 are 'ospf-nbr-full' and both routers advertise their LAN networks), making an ACL drop the most likely cause rather than a routing failure.

plan.problem: Router R1 (router1) has access-list 'R1_IN' applied inbound on Serial0/1/0 with sequence 10 denying ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24, preventing ICMP replies to 192.168.0.11.
plan.fix_summary: Modify ACL 'R1_IN' on router1 to permit ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 on the Serial0/1/0 inbound interface by replacing the denying entry (sequence 10) with a permit for ICMP.

evidence: get_running_config(router1): access_lists.extended entry for 'R1_IN' shows sequence 10 with ace-rule.action = 'permit' and ace-rule.protocol = 'icmp' (previous deny is no longer present).
get_running_config(router1): interface Serial0/1/0 has ip.access-group.in.acl.acl-name = 'R1_IN' (ACL still applied inbound on the R1<->R2 link).
get_interface_status(router1 Serial0/1/0): oper_state = 'up' and input_errors/output_errors = 0 (link operational).
get_ospf(router1): Serial0/1/0 neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.2 state = 'ospf-nbr-full' (control-plane adjacencies healthy).

messages.content:
What was observed:
- Pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 were failing because R1 was dropping ICMP on its Serial0/1/0 link (access-list R1_IN applied inbound).

Diagnosis (root cause):
- ACL R1_IN on router1 had sequence 10 denying ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24, preventing ICMP from 172.16.0.11 reaching the source.

Changes made:
- On router1 replaced the ACL entry at sequence 10 with a permit for ICMP (sequence 10 now permits icmp). The ACL remains applied inbound on Serial0/1/0.

Verification:
- Passed. Running-config shows R1_IN sequence 10 = permit icmp; ACL still applied inbound on Serial0/1/0; Serial0/1/0 is up with no errors; OSPF neighbor to R2 is full.
- Remaining issues: none — ICMP should now pass and pings should succeed.
